findings.title: Velvet Tempest Deploys CastleRAT via ClickFix in Termite Ransomware Campaign
findings.summary: Velvet Tempest (DEV-0504), an affiliate linked to Ryuk, Conti, BlackCat, and LockBit, was observed using ClickFix malvertising to deploy DonutLoader and CastleRAT backdoor in a 12-day campaign against a 3,000-endpoint US non-profit.

findings.title: AirSnitch — Full Bidirectional MitM Attack Against Wi-Fi Encryption
findings.summary: Researchers disclosed AirSnitch, a cross-layer identity desynchronization attack enabling full bidirectional MitM on Wi-Fi networks across homes, offices, and enterprises by exploiting Layer 1/2 binding failures.

findings.title: Phishing Campaigns Abuse .arpa DNS and IPv6 Reverse DNS to Evade Defenses
findings.summary: Threat actors are abusing ip6.arpa reverse DNS zones via Hurricane Electric and Cloudflare to host phishing infrastructure that evades domain reputation checks and email security gateways.

findings.title: Transparent Tribe Uses AI to Mass-Produce Polyglot Malware Implants Targeting India
findings.summary: Pakistan-aligned APT Transparent Tribe adopted AI-assisted coding to produce high-volume disposable implants in Nim, Zig, and Crystal, using Slack, Discord, Supabase, and Google Sheets as C2 channels.

findings.title: CL-UNK-1068 — Chinese Espionage Campaign Targets Asian Critical Infrastructure
findings.affected_technology: Aviation, energy, government, telecom, pharma (South/Southeast/East Asia)
findings.summary: Palo Alto Unit 42 disclosed CL-UNK-1068, a Chinese espionage cluster using custom malware, modified open-source tools, and LOLBINs to target critical infrastructure across aviation, energy, government, telecom, and pharma sectors in Asia.

findings.title: BoryptGrab Stealer Distributed via 100+ Malicious GitHub Repositories
findings.summary: Over 100 GitHub repositories are distributing the BoryptGrab stealer targeting browser data, cryptocurrency wallets, system information, and user files.

noted.summary: Trump administration published new cyber strategy targeting adversaries, critical infrastructure, and emerging technologies including AI and post-quantum cryptography.
Google reported 90 zero-days were exploited in attacks throughout 2025, with nearly half targeting enterprise software.
